title: galanthus nivalis subsp. reginae-olgae
intro: Galanthus nivalis subsp. reginae-olgae, also known as the Queen Olga's snowdrop, is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the snowdrop plant, characterized by its unique flower shape and color. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Greek medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments such as colds, coughs, and bronchitis due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Galanthus nivalis subsp. reginae-olgae include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, reduce fever, and soothe sore throats, making it a valuable herbal remedy for cold and flu season. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea by steeping its dried flowers and leaves in hot water, or it can be used in combination with other herbs to create a syrup or tincture for medicinal purposes.
